title: Bump github.com/containers/image/v5 from 5.10.2 to 5.10.3
body:
Bumps [github.com/containers/image/v5](https://github.com/containers/image) from 5.10.2 to 5.10.3.
Release notes

Sourced from github.com/containers/image/v5's releases.

v5.10.3

  • place shortnames in ~/.cache not ~/.config/.cache
Commits
  • e5b5d45 v5.10.3
  • be981b3 Makefile: set Skopeo branch to release-1.2
  • 9741525 v5.10: place shortnames in ~/.cache not ~/.config/.cache
  • a293c53 bump to v5.10.3-dev
  • See full diff in compare view

[![Dependabot compatibility score](https://api.dependabot.com/badges/compatibility_score?dependency-name=github.com/containers/image/v5&package-manager=go_modules&previous-version=5.10.2&new-version=5.10.3)](https://dependabot.com/compatibility-score/?dependency-name=github.com/containers/image/v5&package-manager=go_modules&previous-version=5.10.2&new-version=5.10.3) Dependabot will resolve any conflicts with this PR as long as you don't alter it yourself. You can also trigger a rebase manually by commenting `@dependabot rebase`. [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-start) [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-end) ---
Dependabot commands and options
You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot merge` will merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot squash and merge` will squash and merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot cancel merge` will cancel a previously requested merge and block automerging - `@dependabot reopen` will reopen this PR if it is closed - `@dependabot close` will close this PR and stop Dependabot recreating it. You can achieve the same result by closing it manually - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot use these labels` will set the current labels as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use these reviewers` will set the current reviewers as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use these assignees` will set the current assignees as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use this milestone` will set the current milestone as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot badge me` will comment on this PR with code to add a "Dependabot enabled" badge to your readme Additionally, you can set the following in your Dependabot [dashboard](https://app.dependabot.com): - Update frequency (including time of day and day of week) - Pull request limits (per update run and/or open at any time) - Out-of-range updates (receive only lockfile updates, if desired) - Security updates (receive only security updates, if desired)

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dependabot-preview[bot]*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Looks like F33 is not yet ready. @giuseppe did crun eat the new `libcap` update already?
Restarted one test to see if F33 has the refreshed libcap
Rebased on HEAD.  I have no idea _why_ the cap failures are happening.  @cevich are we fetching an outdated version of `libcap` in the F33 images?

> are we fetching an outdated version of libcap in the F33 images?

The container images we build are 99% the same as the VM images.  That provides a quick-ish way to find this stuff out:

```
$ podman run -it --rm quay.io/libpod/fedora_podman:c6524344056676352 rpm -q libcap
Trying to pull quay.io/libpod/fedora_podman:c6524344056676352...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob b53dca40d789 done
...cut...
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
libcap-2.26-8.fc33.x86_64
```

(note: it's about a 700mb image download)

> libcap-2.26-8.fc33.x86_64

That's it.  The version is too old.  @cevich, can you build a new one?  I am hesitant as I don't want to break something :^)

> That's it. The version is too old. @cevich, can you build a new one?

Sure.  Actually I'm nearly done bringing in F34 beta as well, would that be okay, or too much change all at once?

>  I am hesitant as I don't want to break something :^)

This is impossible, everything is self-contained in an c/automation_images repo PR.  Nothing escapes until you update the `c12341234` suffix in `.cirrus.yml` (also in a PR).

Updating an existing package is about the easiest new-images you can produce. All that's needed is a dummy-change (like to README.md) in a new PR.  Automation takes care of the rest, spitting out the new image-suffix/tag number at the end.

[Ref: Docs](https://github.com/containers/automation_images#the-last-part-first-overview-step-3)

A newer version of github.com/containers/image/v5 exists, but since this PR has been edited by someone other than Dependabot I haven't updated it. You'll get a PR for the updated version as normal once this PR is merged.

OK, I won't notify you again about this release, but will get in touch when a new version is available. If you'd rather skip all updates until the next major or minor version, let me know by commenting `@dependabot ignore this major version` or `@dependabot ignore this minor version`.

If you change your mind, just re-open this PR and I'll resolve any conflicts on it.
